conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

First, could you please tell me: **Do you have a child or children that you're responsible for?** If yes, how many children are you asking about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do have a child I'm responsible for. I have one child - my daughter Alex, she's 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Let me ask you the next question:

**Are you responsible for Alex?** By this I mean, do you live with her and are you the person who is mainly responsible for her care (such as providing food, clothing, and looking after her day-to-day)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. Alex lives with me and I'm the one who looks after her day-to-day - I provide all her meals, buy her clothes, take her to school, and take care of all her needs.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great, thank you for confirming that.

Next question: **Where do you live?** Specifically, are you living in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm based in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Now I need to ask: **Is Alex under 16 years old, or if she's 16 or over, is she in approved education or training?**

Since you mentioned Alex is 8 years old, she would meet the age requirement, but I just want to confirm there are no other special circumstances.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's right, Alex is 8 years old, so she's under 16. She just goes to primary school like any other child her age. No special circumstances or anything like that.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment.

**Does anyone else also claim Child Benefit for Alex?** For example, does Alex's other parent or anyone else already receive Child Benefit for her?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. It's just me looking after her and I'm the only one who would be claiming.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that. One final question:

**Is Alex currently living away from home in any of these situations:**
- In hospital for more than 12 weeks
- In local authority care or detention
- Living with someone else who is not claiming Child Benefit for her

Or does she live with you full-time at home?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: She lives with me full-time at home. She's not in hospital or care or anything like that - she's just at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing all that information. Based on everything you've told me, I can now assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:
- Alex is 8 years old (under 16)
- You live in the UK
- You are responsible for Alex and she lives with you full-time
- You provide all her day-to-day care
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for her
- She is not in hospital or local authority care

You meet all the requirements under UK Child Benefit rules. You can proceed with making a claim for Child Benefit.

**Please note:** If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, but you can still claim Child Benefit.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex

circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit: 1) She is under 16 years old (8 years old). 2) You are responsible for her - you live with her and provide her day-to-day care including food, clothing, and other necessities. 3) You live in the UK (Manchester). 4) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. 5) Alex lives with you full-time at home and is not in hospital for more than 12 weeks or in local authority care. According to gov.uk guidance, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK, and you'll usually be responsible for a child if you live with them. All these conditions are met for Alex.

The claimant lives in the UK, is responsible for Alex (age 8), lives with her full-time, provides all her day-to-day care, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for her. All eligibility criteria under UK Child Benefit rules are satisfied.
